book_summary: Matthew 1-28 has full chapter coverage with source-audited-draft restored translation and mystical companion layers. Greek terms are contextually rendered with source hooks: metanoia as turn/realign, basileia as reign/realm, Gehenna preserved, aion/aionios as age/age-long, dikaiosyne as righteousness/justice, psyche as life/soul, sozo as saved/healed, parousia as arrival/presence, and synteleia tou aionos as completion of the age.

Magi, Herod, star, Bethlehem/Micah, Egypt flight, Rachel lament, return to Nazareth, dream guidance and empire violence.
John the Baptist; metanoeite as turn/realign, reign of heavens, Jordan baptism, fruit worthy of realignment, Spirit/fire, Jesus baptism and beloved Son voice.
Wilderness testing, adversarial/testing language, scripture replies, realms/glory temptation, Galilee light citation, first disciples, teaching/proclamation/healing summary.

Sign demand, leaven teaching, Peter confession, rock/assembly/keys, first passion prediction, Peter as adversarial stumbling-block, psyche as life/soul.

translation_notes: Greek metanoeite/metanoia should be rendered contextually as turn/realign/change consciousness rather than flattened to guilt-heavy repent.
Basileia ton ouranon should be handled as reign/realm of the heavens, not merely a distant place called heaven.
Gehenna should remain Gehenna with notes, not flattened into generic hell.
Aiōn language should be rendered age/era where context supports it, not world by default.
Dikaiosyne should be translated by context as justice/righteousness/right-relatedness rather than only moral legal status.
Jesus' teaching layer should preserve Jewish apocalyptic, wisdom, and Torah context while keeping mystical companion interpretation separate.
Matthew 4 adversarial language is rendered contextually as tester/adversarial one/adversary according to the scene, with Greek source hooks retained under the terms layer.

Matthew 5-7 preserves Sermon on the Mount as Jewish wisdom/Torah fulfillment teaching rather than anti-Torah replacement. Gehenna remains Gehenna, teleios is rendered complete/whole, and the Father's reign is practical earth-facing alignment.
Matthew 8-14 preserves sozo as saved/healed where the context bridges rescue and embodied healing, psyche as life/soul, aion as age, and ego eimi as I am in the sea-walking scene.
Spirit/deliverance passages use careful language: unclean spirits / troubling forces / spirit-language, avoiding over-importing later demonology while preserving the narrative function.

Matthew 15-21 preserves heart/mouth purity logic, contextual adversary language, ekklesia as assembly, palingenesia as renewal/regeneration, aionios as age-long, and fruit as the key sign of alignment.

Matthew 22-28 preserves final controversy, apocalyptic, passion, and resurrection language with source hooks: parousia as arrival/presence, synteleia tou aionos as completion of the age, kolasis as correction, covenant blood as release, and commission as discipling the nations.
